name: microsoft-kiota-abstractions

metadata.name: microsoft-kiota-abstractions
metadata.summary: Core abstractions for kiota generated libraries in Python

documentation:
# Microsoft Kiota Abstractions Library for Python

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/microsoft-kiota-abstractions.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/microsoft-kiota-abstractions)
[![Downloads](https://pepy.tech/badge/microsoft-kiota-abstractions)](https://pepy.tech/project/microsoft-kiota-abstractions)

The Microsoft Kiota abstractions library for Python is the python package defining the basic constructs Kiota projects need once an SDK has been generated from an OpenAPI definition.

A [Kiota](https://github.com/microsoft/kiota) generated project will need a reference to the abstraction package to build and run.

Read more about Kiota [here](https://github.com/microsoft/kiota/blob/main/README.md).

## Using the Abstractions Library

In order to use this library, install the package by running:

```cmd
pip install microsoft-kiota-abstractions
```

## Contributing

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions.  Most contributions require you to agree to a
Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us
the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit <https://cla.opensource.microsoft.com>.

When you submit a pull request, a CLA bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide
a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (e.g., status check, comment). Simply follow the instructions
provided by the bot. You will only need to do this once across all repos using our CLA.

This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/).
For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/) or
contact [opencode@microsoft.com](mailto:opencode@microsoft.com) with any additional questions or comments.

## Trademarks

This project may contain trademarks or logos for projects, products, or services. Authorized use of Microsoft
trademarks or logos is subject to and must follow
[Microsoft's Trademark & Brand Guidelines](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/legal/intellectualproperty/trademarks/usage/general).
Use of Microsoft trademarks or logos in modified versions of this project must not cause confusion or imply Microsoft sponsorship.
Any use of third-party trademarks or logos are subject to those third-party's policies.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store_factory_singleton.BackingStoreFactorySingleton.__new__
documentation:
Creates a single instance of the class during instantiation. It also
ensures that the backing store factory that was set in the first
instantion of the class will be the default during run time.

Licensed under the MIT License.
See License in the project root for license information.

classes.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.get
documentation:
Gets a value from the backing store based on its key. Returns null if the value hasn't
changed and "ReturnOnlyChangedValues" is true.

Args:
    key (str): The key to lookup the backing store with.

Returns:
    Optional[T]: The value from the backing store.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.set
documentation:
Sets or updates the stored value for the given key. Will trigger subscriptions callbacks.

Args:
    key (str): The key to store and retrieve the information.
    value (T): The value to be stored.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.enumerate_
documentation:
Enumerates all the values stored in the backing store. Values will be filtered if
"ReturnOnlyChangedValues" is true.

Returns:
    List[Tuple[str, Any]]: The values available in the backing store.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.enumerate_keys_for_values_changed_to_null
documentation:
Enumerates the keys for all values that changed to null.

Returns:
    List[str]: The keys for the values that changed to null.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.subscribe
documentation:
Creates a subscription to any data change happening.

Args:
    callback (Callable[[str, Any, Any], None]):Callback to be invoked on data changes
    where the first parameter is the data key, the second the previous value and the third
    the new value.
    subscription_id (Optional[str]): The subscription Id to use.

Returns:
    str: The subscription Id to use when removing the subscription

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.unsubscribe
documentation:
Removes a subscription from the store based on its subscription id.

Args:
    subscription_id (str): The Id of the subscription to remove.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.clear
documentation: Clears the data stored in the backing store. Doesn't trigger any subscription.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.is_initialization_completed
documentation:
Whether the initialization of the object and/or the initial deserialization has been
completed to track whether objects have changed.

Returns:
    bool:

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.is_initialization_completed
documentation:
Sets the initialization completed state of the object.

Args:
    completed (bool): Whether the initialization of the object and/or the initial
    deserialization has been completed to track whether objects have changed.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.return_only_changed_values
documentation:
Whether to return only values that have changed since the initialization of the object
when calling the Get and Enumerate methods.

Returns:
    bool:

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.backing_store.BackingStore.return_only_changed_values
documentation:
Sets whether to return only values that have changed since the initialization of the
object when calling the Get and Enumerate methods.

Args:
    changed (bool): Whether to return only values that have changed since the
    initialization of the object when calling the Get and Enumerate methods.

Stores model information in a different location than the object properties.
Implementations can provide dirty tracking capabilities, caching capabilities
or integration with 3rd party stores

functions.name: kiota_abstractions.utils.lazy_import
documentation:
Lazily imports a python module given its absolute path.

Note: This a utility method for use in Kiota generated code only and not
meant for application outside of that scenario.

Args:
    name (str): Absolute path to the module

Returns:
    module: The module to be imported

Sets the specified object with the given key in the store.

Args:

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.in_memory_backing_store.InMemoryBackingStore.enumerate_
documentation:
Enumerate the values in the store based on the ReturnOnlyChangedValues configuration
value

Returns:

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.in_memory_backing_store.InMemoryBackingStore.subscribe
documentation:
Adds a callback to subscribe to events in the store with the given subscription id

Args:
    callback (Callable[[str, Any, Any], None]): The callback to add
    subscription_id (Optional[str]): The subscription id to use for subscription

Returns:
    str: The id of the subscription

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.in_memory_backing_store.InMemoryBackingStore.unsubscribe
documentation:
De-register the callback with the given subscriptionId

Args:
    subscription_id (str): The id of the subscription to de-register

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.in_memory_backing_store.InMemoryBackingStore.clear
documentation: Clears the store

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.store.in_memory_backing_store.InMemoryBackingStore._ensure_collection_size_is_consistent
documentation:
Checks if entry of type collection has changed in size.
If so, dirty tracks the change by calling set()

Args:
    key (str): _description_
    entry(StoreEntry): _description_
In-memory implementation of the backing store. Allows for dirty tracking of changes.

classes.name: kiota_abstractions.authentication.allowed_hosts_validator.AllowedHostsValidator

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.authentication.allowed_hosts_validator.AllowedHostsValidator.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new AllowedHostsValidator object with provided values.

Args:
    allowed_hosts (List[str]): A list of valid hosts.  If the list is empty, all hosts

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.authentication.allowed_hosts_validator.AllowedHostsValidator.get_allowed_hosts
documentation:
Gets the list of valid hosts.  If the list is empty, all hosts are valid.

Returns:

Sets the list of valid hosts.  If the list is empty, all hosts are valid.

documentation:
Checks whether the provided host is valid.

Args:
    url (str): The url to check.

Returns:
    bool: [description]

Maintains a list of valid hosts and allows authentication providers to check whether
a host is valid before authenticating a request

documentation:
Gets the header values corresponding to a specific header name.

Args:

Returns:

documentation:
Get all headers and values stored so far.

Returns:

documentation:
Get header values corresponding to a specific header.

Args:

Returns:

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.try_add
documentation:
Adds values to the header with the specified name if it's not already present

Args:
    header_name (str): The name of the header to add values to.
    header_value (str): The values to add to the header.

Returns:
    bool: If the header value has been added to headers.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.add_all
documentation:
Adds the specified headers to the collection.

Args:
    headers (Dict[str, str]): The headers to add.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.add
documentation:
Adds values to the header with the specified name.

Args:
    header_name (str): The name of the header to add values to.
    header_values (List[str]): The values to add to the header.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.keys
documentation:
Gets the header names present in the collection.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.count
documentation: Gets the number of headers present in the collection.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.remove_value
documentation:
Removes the specified value from the header with the specified name.

Args:
    header_name (str): The name of the header to remove the value from.
    header_value (str): The value to remove from the header.

Returns:
    bool: _description_

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.remove
documentation:
Removes the header with the specified name.

Args:
    header_name (str): The name of the header to remove.

Returns:
    bool: True if the header has been removed, False otherwise.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.clear
documentation: Removes all headers from the collection.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.headers_collection.HeadersCollection.contains
documentation:
Checks whether the collection contains a specific header.

Args:
    key (str): The name of the header to check for.

Returns:
    bool: True if the header is present, false otherwise.

classes.name: kiota_abstractions.multipart_body.MultipartBody

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.multipart_body.MultipartBody.add_or_replace_part
documentation:
Adds or replaces a part to the multipart body.

Args:
    part_name (str): The name of the part to add or replace.
    content_type (str): The content type of the part.
    part_value (T): The value of the part.
    filename (str, optional): The filename of the part.

Returns:
    None

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.multipart_body.MultipartBody.get_part_value
documentation: Gets the value of a part from the multipart body.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.multipart_body.MultipartBody.remove_part
documentation:
Removes a part from the multipart body.

Args:
    part_name (str): The name of the part to remove.

Returns:
    bool: True if the part was removed, False otherwise.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.multipart_body.MultipartBody.get_field_deserializers
documentation:
Gets the deserialization information for this object.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Callable[[ParseNode], None]]: The deserialization information for this
    object where each entry is a property key with its deserialization callback.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.multipart_body.MultipartBody.serialize
documentation:
Writes the objects properties to the current writer.

Args:
    writer (SerializationWriter): The writer to write to.

Represents a multipart body for a request or a response.
Example usage:
    multipart = MultipartBody()
    multipart.add_or_replace_part(
        "file", "image/jpeg", open("image.jpg", "rb").read(), "image.jpg"
    )
    multipart.add_or_replace_part("text", "text/plain", "Hello, World!")
    with open("output.txt", "w") as output_file:
        multipart.serialize(output_file)

classes.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter.get_serialization_writer_factory
documentation:
Gets the serialization writer factory currently in use for the HTTP core service.

Returns:
    SerializationWriterFactory: the serialization writer factory currently in use for the
    HTTP core service.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter.send_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized response model.

Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): the request info to execute.
    parsable_factory (ParsableFactory): the class of response model to
        deserialize the response into.
    error_map (Optional[Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]]): the error dict to use in case
    of a failed request.

Returns:
    ModelType: the deserialized response model.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter.send_collection_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized response model collection.

Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): the request info to execute.
    parsable_factory (ParsableFactory): the class of response model to
        deserialize the response into.
    error_map (Optional[Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]]): the error dict to use in
    case of a failed request.

Returns:
    ModelType: the deserialized response model collection.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter.send_collection_of_primitive_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized response model collection.

Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): the request info to execute.
    response_type (PrimitiveType): the class of the response model to deserialize the
    response into.
    error_map (Optional[Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]]): the error dict to use in
    case of a failed request.

Returns:
    Optional[List[PrimitiveType]]: The deserialized primitive collection.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter.send_primitive_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized primitive response model.

Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): the request info to execute.
    response_type (str): the class name of the response model to deserialize the
    response into.
    error_map (Optional[Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]]): the error dict to use in
    case of a failed request.

Returns:
    ResponseType: the deserialized primitive response model.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter.send_no_response_content_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized primitive response model.

Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation):the request info to execute.
    error_map (Optional[Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]]): the error dict to use in
    case of a failed request.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter.enable_backing_store
documentation:
Enables the backing store proxies for the SerializationWriters and ParseNodes in use.

Args:
    backing_store_factory (Optional[BackingStoreFactory]): the backing store factory to use.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.request_adapter.RequestAdapter.convert_to_native_async
documentation:
Translates the request information object into a native HTTP client request object.

Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): request information object to be converted.

Returns:
    RequestType: the natively typed HTTP request of the client.
Service responsible for translating abstract Request Info into concrete native HTTP requests.

methods.name: kiota_abstractions.native_response_handler.NativeResponseHandler.handle_response_async
documentation:
Callback method that is invoked when a response is received.
Args:
    response (NativeResponseType): The type of the native response object.
    error_map (Optional[Dict[str, ParsableFactory]]): the error dict to use
    in case of a failed request.
